commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add mm/inches unit setting with live DetailWindow updates

- Add Measurement Units submenu (mm / inches) to Settings menu
- Persist and sync unit preference across all windows
- DetailWindow Calibrated Sheet mode now respects the unit setting:
  - Reference size input label updates to mm or in
  - User's calibration input converted from in→mm internally (px/mm stays canonical)
  - D50/D90 results display in the selected unit
  - Histogram x-axis labels and unit tag update on the fly
- Switching units resets calibration and clears stale results

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

commit.message:
Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)
- E4: vertical scale for all modes (was shift+E3)
- E5: gain/normalize in trim/loop, threshold in slice auto (was E4)

- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function

- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Honesty overhaul: rewrite all portfolio content to accurately reflect my skills and contributions

Every page now clearly states what I built vs. what AI built.

Homepage: "CS Graduate" instead of "Software Engineer", removed "full-stack developer" claims.
About: Rewritten as honest career-changer story, skills section renamed "Skills I Can Defend in an Interview".
Resume (data, page, markdown, PDF): Matches actual March 2026 resume — removed Redis/BullMQ/Socket.io/Kubernetes/Jenkins/GitHub Actions, removed UX Design minor, updated education and experience to match PDF exactly.
Now page: Updated to current priorities.

Project pages rewritten:
- TrackMate: honest about team contributions, not solo
- Adventure Game: clear that original Python is my code, Django version is AI-expanded and incomplete
- Vendor Manager: honest that AI wrote advanced features, MERN rebuild is separate AI-scaffolded project
- Where's My App: marked as incomplete concept, AI wrote most of the code
- Digital Portfolio: AI helped with CSS/design, content is mine
- Home Server: expanded as strongest asset — 44 containers, all my work
- Property Comparison: un-drafted, honest about AI authorship but highlights real deployment and daily use
- Community Hub: new page for CS 3620 Django project (private repo)
- Shed Generator: drafted (hidden) — 100% AI-written
- 1980 Adventure Game: updated to match honest framing

Removed from skills: Redis, BullMQ, Socket.io, Kubernetes, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Shadcn/ui, Zustand, and other technologies only present in AI-written code.
Removed from education: UX Design minor, Certificate of Proficiency, AS in General Studies.
Featured projects updated: Home Server first, replaced Where's My App with Home Server.

Füge vollständigen Admin-Bereich hinzu

Frontend:
- Neue Admin-Dashboard-Komponente mit 4 Tabs

Features:
- Admin-Zugriff nur für Benutzer mit role='admin'
- Admin-Button im Dashboard für Admin-Benutzer
- Route /admin für Admin-Dashboard
- User-Rollen werden bei Login/Register zurückgegeben

Fix Top Ideas: don't cache empty results, remove debug logs

Empty scan results were being cached, preventing future auto-scans

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch user_code_deployment_type from DOCKER to K8S

ECS and K8S are the known container-based hybrid agent types in Dagster
Cloud. Using DOCKER likely caused Dagster Cloud to omit user-defined
deployment env vars from cloud_context_env. K8S is the closest match
for a container-based hybrid agent without a dedicated ACA type.

commit.message:
spec(050): rewrite intent prompt packs spec to v1 post-interview

Decisions locked:
- Default intent = strategy (no --intent flag = strategy pack)
- Ordering: pack prompts first (lex), then --prompts appended
- prompts/analysis.md moved into packs/strategy/00_overview.md; original deleted
- Missing/empty pack + --prompts = warn + continue; no --prompts = exit 2
- Placeholders: {{query}}, {{intent}}, {{deliverables}}; unknowns left unchanged
- No short aliases; full names only (strategy, implementation, deliverables)
- Full skeleton content for all 7 pack files included in spec

commit.message:
Fix tool bridge: append result before escalation break + add fallback

Move tool result message append before the large-result escalation break
so the result is always in the messages list regardless of code path.

truncated tool result to the small LLM for a best-effort summary
instead of returning empty/stale content.

commit.message:
fix(makefile): detect ANY variable override, not just IMG

Use $(origin VAR) to detect if any image-related variable was
overridden from command line or environment. This fixes the issue
where IMG_TAG override was still applying git tag logic.

Previous logic:
  ifeq ($(IMG),$(REGISTRY)/$(IMG_NAME):$(IMG_TAG))
Problem: This is always TRUE even when IMG_TAG is overridden,
because both sides of the equation use the same overridden value.

New logic:
  ifeq ($(origin IMG)$(origin IMG_TAG)$(origin REGISTRY)$(origin IMG_NAME),filefilefilefile)
Solution: Checks if ALL variables are from Makefile (file).
If ANY variable is from 'command line' or 'environment', skip git tag.

Behavior:
1. All defaults:
   make podman-build
   → tags: latest (and git tag if present)

2. ANY override (IMG_TAG, IMG, REGISTRY, IMG_NAME):
   make podman-build IMG_TAG=v1.0.0-test2
   → tags: ONLY v1.0.0-test2 (git tag SKIPPED)

   make podman-push IMG_TAG=v1.0.0-test2
   → pushes: ONLY v1.0.0-test2 (git tag SKIPPED)

This ensures that when you specify an override, you get EXACTLY
what you asked for, no extra tags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase D: Auth + API routes with dual auth support

Extension auth:
- Google OAuth via chrome.identity.launchWebAuthFlow
- Supabase session stored in chrome.storage.local with auto-refresh
- getSession/getAuthToken/signOut helpers

Web app auth:
- Login page with Google SSO + Magic Link email
- OAuth callback route exchanging code for session
- Root page redirects based on auth state

API routes (all with CORS + OPTIONS):
- POST /api/captures: accepts payload with base64 images, uploads to
  Supabase Storage, inserts DB row, returns capture ID
- GET /api/captures: paginated list with user-scoped RLS
- GET /api/captures/[id]: single capture fetch
- DELETE /api/captures/[id]: removes DB row + storage assets
- Dual auth: Bearer token (extension) or cookie session (web app)

Database schema SQL for captures table with RLS + index.

commit.message:
fix: visit_detected IDs cross-référencés depuis contacts_matched

L'IA match correctement les contacts dans contacts_matched mais n'injecte
pas toujours leur ID dans visit_detected.buyer/seller_lead_id. Résultat :
les dataset du visitBlock restaient vides → condition (sellerLeadId ||
buyerLeadId) false → visite non créée en DB → aucune sync calendrier.

Fix : après avoir stocké les IDs depuis vd, on parcourt contacts_matched
pour remplir les IDs manquants du visitBlock (buyer et seller).

feat: add PM/UX negotiation, deferred items, and QA→PM escalation

CLAUDE.md pipeline changes:
- Refinement: PM + UX now negotiate — accept, defer, or reject each
  UX recommendation instead of silently incorporating
- QA: new AC_PROBLEM path escalates to PM when acceptance criteria
  are the issue (not code). PM negotiates updates, deferred items
  go to backlog
- Deferred Items: any item deferred during negotiation gets a new
  backlog issue via /idea with a comment linking back to the original

QA skill changes:
- Added AC_PROBLEM as a third verdict alongside PASS/FAIL
- AC_PROBLEM leaves issue in Testing for orchestrator to route to PM

commit.message:
fix: all 6 cash tables always visible + bot redistribution + stats fixes

- runTableSession: remove activeBotTables.delete() from finally block so
  tables without bots (Micro, High Stakes, Nosebleed, Heads Up) stay
  visible in /api/tables between sessions instead of disappearing
- CASH_TABLES: redistribute bots — 2 bots per table instead of 4 in 2:
  Low=wolf/owl, Mid=shark/rock, High=mago/caos, Nosebleed=turtle/fox
- /api/stats onlineAgents: use agentBridge.listAgents().length (all
  registered agents) instead of listOnlineAgents() (WS-only), so HTTP
  agents count toward ONLINE NOW
- /api/stats totalHands: use max(DB, in-memory) so real-time hand count
  is always at least as high as the in-memory counter even when DB lags

commit.message:
[agency] bugfix-mobile-navbar (#109)

* fix: remove text labels from mobile bottom nav buttons

The mobile bottom navigation rendered <span> text labels ("Libraries",
"Discover", "Chat", etc.) below each icon. On small screens this crowded
the 64px bar and made the labels feel oversized.

Remove the visible <span> labels and the now-orphaned `.mobileNavItem span`
CSS rule. Each button already carries an aria-label, so screen reader
accessibility is preserved. This matches the icon-only convention used by
iOS tab bars, Android bottom nav, and most major mobile apps.

Desktop sidebar navigation is unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add regression test for icon-only mobile nav buttons

Verifies two behavioral properties of the mobile bottom navigation:

1. No visible text labels — queryByText returns null for every nav label
   ("Libraries", "Discover", "Chat", "Search", "Settings", "Tabs").
2. Accessible names preserved — getByRole("button", { name }) still
   resolves each button via aria-label.

Uses Testing Library queries exclusively (no direct DOM access) to stay
lint-clean and assert user-visible behavior per project testing standards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
